description_long: This object is a large, industrial control cabinet system, typically found in urban infrastructure settings such as power distribution sites or utility management areas. It features a robust, rectangular metal housing with four vertical access panels or doors, each likely corresponding to separate control or power modules. The upper section contains a digital display and several labeled indicator panels, suggesting monitoring and operational control functions. The base is supported by a tubular frame, providing stability and possibly cable management. The exterior is primarily gray with green and white accent labels, giving it a utilitarian and modern appearance. The overall design is modular, with clear separation between compartments for safety and maintenance.
description: A large, modular industrial control cabinet with four vertical access panels, digital display, and green/white indicator labels, housed in a sturdy gray metal frame.
description_view_0: Front view showing four vertical access panels, digital display, and labeled indicator panels.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the cabinet and the tubular support frame.
description_view_2: Rear view with the back of the access panels and the tubular frame visible, no display or labels.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the cabinet's depth and frame structure.
